heading: Action by President with respect to affirmative determination
label: 4574 Action by President with respect to affirmative determination
notes: References in TextThis part, referred to in subsec. (e), was in the original “this subtitle”, meaning subtitle C (§§ 321–327) of title III of , , , which is classified principally to this subchapter. For complete classification of subtitle C to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 116–113134 Stat. 54Jan. 29, 2020

Delegation of FunctionsProc. No. 10053, par. (13), , 85 F.R. 39826, authorized the United States Trade Representative, after consultation with the Secretary of Transportation, to exercise the authority of the President under this section.June 29, 2020
amendments: References in TextThis part, referred to in subsec. (e), was in the original “this subtitle”, meaning subtitle C (§§ 321–327) of title III of , , , which is classified principally to this subchapter. For complete classification of subtitle C to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 116–113134 Stat. 54Jan. 29, 2020

children.text: the President shall, subject to subsection (b), issue an order to the Secretary of Transportation specifying the relief to be provided, consistent with subsection (c), and directing the relief to be carried out; and
the Secretary of Transportation shall carry out such relief.
is not in the national economic interest of the United States; or
would cause serious harm to the national security of the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: With respect to a determination relating to an investigation under , the denial or imposition of limitations on a request for a new grant of authority by the persons of Mexico that are the subject of the investigation.section 4572(a)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: With respect to a determination relating to an investigation under , the revocation of, or restrictions on, grants of authority issued to the persons of Mexico that are the subject of the investigation since the date of the petition, request, or resolution.section 4572(a)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: With respect to a determination relating to an investigation under section 4572(a)(2) or (3) of this title, the revocation or imposition of limitations on an existing grant of authority by the persons of Mexico that are the subject of the investigation.

children.label: C
children.text: With respect to a determination relating to an investigation under section 4572(a)(1), (2), or (3) of this title, a cap on the number of grants of authority issued to persons of Mexico annually.

children.heading: Deadline for relief
children.label: 2 Deadline for relief
children.text: Not later than 15 days after the date on which the President determines the relief to be provided under this subsection, the President shall direct the Secretary of Transportation to carry out the relief.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), any relief that the President provides under this section may not be in effect for more than 2 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (C), the President, after receiving a determination from the Commission under subparagraph (B) that is affirmative, or which contains a determination that the President may treat as affirmative in accordance with , may extend the effective period of relief provided under this section by up to an additional 4 years, if the President determines that the provision of the relief continues to be necessary to remedy or prevent material harm.section 1330(d)(1) of this title

children.heading: Investigation
children.label: i Investigation
children.text: Upon request of the President, or upon the filing by an interested party described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of which is representative of a United States long-haul trucking services industry that is filed with the Commission not earlier than the date that is 270 days, and not later than the date that is 240 days, before the date on which any action taken under this section is to terminate, the Commission shall conduct an investigation to determine whether action under this section continues to be necessary to remedy or prevent material harm.section 4571(8) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: publish notice of the commencement of an investigation under clause (i) in the Federal Register; and

children.label: II
children.text: within a reasonable time thereafter, hold a public hearing at which the Commission shall afford interested parties an opportunity to be present, to present evidence, and to respond to the presentations of other parties and consumers, and otherwise be heard.

children.heading: Report
children.label: iii Report
children.text: Not later than the date that is 60 days before relief provided under subsection (a) is to terminate, or such other date as determined by the President, the Commission shall submit to the President a report on its investigation and determination under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Period of relief
children.label: C Period of relief
children.text: Any relief provided under this section, including any extension thereof, may not, in the aggregate, be in effect for more than 6 years.

children.label: I
children.text: the subject matter of the investigation is the same as the subject matter of a previous investigation conducted under subparagraph (B)(i); and

children.label: II
children.text: less than 1 year has elapsed since the Commission made its report to the President of the results of such previous investigation.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply with respect to an investigation if the Commission determines good cause exists to conduct the investigation.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: e Regulations
children.text: The Commission and the Secretary of Transportation are authorized to promulgate such rules and regulations as may be necessary to carry out this part.
